fixture_insights.insight: Alternate strong chip strategies depend on team strength and remaining chips: free hit 33 wild card 35, or wildcard 33 free hit 34, etc.
Approximately 45% of top managers are on a wildcard for GW32, while 41% are saving it for later.
Arsenal assets are generally being deprioritized for this week, with plans to bring back from GW35.
Arsenal defensive assets (Raya, Gabriel, Saliba) very good for coming fixtures.
Avoid Chelsea defense due to cost and lack of certainty. Focus on strong, cheap defenders from doubling teams.
Bench and transfer planning is essential—having 4-5 transfers allows late pivots that mirror a late wild card team without needing to wild card late.
Bench Boost and Free Hit strategies are being deployed in alternate manners (e.g., BB32/FH33 vs. WC32/BB33).
Bench Boost can be effective in GW32 if team is set up with many strong home fixtures, or in DGW33 for doublers.
Bench Boost in 33 offers the strongest bench this season, especially compared to BB35/36 setups.
Bench boost is best in GW33 if possible (most doubles), but alternatives exist in GW35/36/37 for single gameweek teams.
Bench boost is most valuable in double GW (GW33), but can be tweaked if team suits other timings or if chip not available.
Bench boost strategy requires maximizing doublers for 33, so wildcards should target those teams.
Bench order and start/sit questions approached methodically, with advice to favor attackers in form even with tricky fixtures.
Bench order should reflect form and likely minutes over fixture alone.
Bench ranking for GW32 provided (GK and attackers).
Brighton: Pascal Gross is safest midfield pick; Welbeck, Hinchelwood, Mitoma also options. Avoid Gomez (suspension risk), Ma (low mins).
Bruno is the best captain: multiple routes to points, safe minutes, fixtures, set-pieces, penalties.
Buying Palmer and Tava is based on form and role, but fixtures are 'horrendous' this week, so some suggest holding/selling then Free Hit later for better returns.
Captaincy: Bruno or Haaland preferred; Bowen, Thiago, Salah secondary differentials.
Captaincy is clear: Bruno Fernandes best, followed by Haaland, Thiago, and Bowen.
Cautious advice for those without wild card/free hit: target long-term reliable players (City, Palace, etc.), not just doublers.
Chip plan: Wildcard 32, Bench Boost 33, Free Hit 34.
Crystal Palace potential rotation in GW36 means avoid overinvesting in that double; Conference League progress discussed.
Crystal Palace rotation risk in GW36 due to Conference League (be wary of triple-up).
Defensive choices for Arsenal, Brighton, Leeds rank highly for upcoming fixtures; safety of starts and fixture run are weighed heavily.
Different strategies outlined in detail for all likely chip situations (all chips, no wild card, no bench boost, etc.)—must assess your own squad.
Differential picks like Mitoma benefit from strong fixture (Burnley).
Differentials like Mitoma and Gross (Brighton) are recommended, especially with good fixtures (Burnley).
Ekit is the most sold this week due to doubts about minutes and Champions League rotation.
Ekit sold due to heavy game schedule/rotation risk, as Liverpool has UCL matches and others may return to starting XI.
Ekitike a sell due to minutes risk; Bowen an upgrade.
Ekitiké is a fitness/minutes risk with upcoming matches; selling for a doubler like Welbeck/Calvert-Lewin is advisable unless strong news he starts.
Emphasis on not picking Chelsea defenders or spending heavily on defense except for key options.
Fixture uncertainty for doubles and blanks, especially for teams like Man City, Palace, Chelsea, Newcastle.
Forwards are weak this week but necessary for team structure and future doubles; prioritize team needs according to chip plan.
Frontline of Haaland, João Pedro, and Calvert-Lewin (or Welbeck) is suggested, although forwards do not look strong for GW32 specifically.
Gabrielle, Bruno Fernandes, and Cole Palmer are key holds.
Gordon is good, but a Brighton midfielder (Gross) is preferred due to doubles and money structure.
GW32 Wildcard lets you maximize GW33 bench boost (most doubles), then Free Hit covers GW34 blank, then Triple Captain in anticipated double GW36 (City/Palace).
Heavy buy/sell activity on Palmer and Joao Pedro due to chip strategy differences.
If team is already strong this week, can delay wild card to GW35 to catch Crystal Palace double in 36 and strong season run-in; Free Hit 33 if needed for doublers.
Key defenders include O'Reilly, Gabriel, Brighton and Leeds options; Chelsea defenders are not prioritized.
Leeds: Darlow good GK, Pascal Struijk safest DEF (Rodon/Bejol risk), Ampadu decent DEF, Aaronson unreliable mins up front.
Leeds defense (Darlow) is cheap and enables Bench Boost.
Likely to free hit in GW33, wildcard in GW35.
Liverpool's schedule causes likely rotation, downgrading their assets' appeal for GW32.
Main assets have strong doubles or are key attackers from high-upside teams.
Midfielders Palmer, Semenyo, Bruno Fernandes, and Tavernier are near-essential.
Minute risks (e.g., Ekitike) and suspension/injury risks (Gomez, Rodon, Bejol) are flagged as avoidances.
Mostly satisfied with current squad for GW32; no urgent transfers needed.
Not worried about not having triple Arsenal or triple Palace for later weeks.
Not worried by not having triple Arsenal or Palace; can bring them in later if needed.
One free transfer may be used, but likely to save the other for flexibility after the break.
Plans to manage transfers into Gameweeks 36/37 for any missing Palace or Arsenal players.
Player buys for different draft strategies summarized by position and chip route (see recommendations).
Player recommendations focus on maximizing doublers for GW33, and securing long-term picks (City, Palace, Arsenal) for non-chip users.
Preference for bringing in Tottenham, Fulham, Man United, or possibly West Ham assets over others.
Prefers a Brighton midfielder (likely Gross) over Gordon for doubles and price flexibility.
Prefers this chip plan for less injury/rotation risk close to BB, and to optimize for the remaining chips.
Punt picks in midfield (Matoma, Hinchwood, Gross) add upside to an otherwise template wildcard.
Punts on Matoma, Hinchwood, or Gross are suggested for midfield fifth spot.
Risk of post-break injuries and international minutes prompts conservative transfer usage.
Rotation likely for Liverpool due to fixture congestion.
Sees little advantage in WC35/BB36 or saving wildcard for later.
Several player transfer moves discussed in depth for managers not wildcarding, with attention to chip strategy.
Some advocate for avoiding Arsenal players now, as they don’t double soon; plan to re-acquire them later (GW35).
Targeting players with double gameweeks for maximized points potential.
Team selections heavily favor players doubling in GW33.
Top targets include Tava, Palmer, and Leeds assets (noted for strong DGW33 fixtures).
Tottenham and Fulham, and possibly Man United assets have favorable fixtures and less rotation risk.
Transfer moves for non-wildcarders are best justified for maximizing doublers or if the outgoing player is injured or a rotation risk.
Triple captain GW36 (likely City/Palace double) preferred if chip is available, with fallback single GW38 for Salah (if minutes expected).
Typical chip strategy seeks to maximize doubling assets, but depends if team is strong for GW32—if so, delay wildcard.
Waiting for fixture announcements before bringing in West Ham players.
Wildcard activated in Gameweek 32 to set up Bench Boost in 33.
Wildcard GW32 draft: Vugan/Darlow GK, O'Reilly/Van Heck/Gabriel/Senesi/James Hill DEF, Pascal Gross/Semenyo/Cole Palmer/Bruno/Tavernier MID, J Pedro/Haaland/Calvert-Lewin FWD.
Wildcard GW32 is the most explosive route if all chips remain (allows bench boost GW33, free hit GW34, triple captain GW36).
Wildcard in GW32 is primarily for managers planning to bench boost in GW33 and free hit in GW34.
Wildcard now allows for flexibility and holding value players like Palmer, Gabriel.
Wildcard now can set up well for DGW33 and exploit favorable Leeds fixtures.
Wildcard timing is split; it depends on your team setup and upcoming double/blank gameweeks.
Xiao Pedro included reluctantly for his double, although confidence in him is low.
